days.title: World Wetlands Day
days.description: Marks the 1971 adoption of the Ramsar Convention, promoting conservation of wetland ecosystems worldwide.

days.title: International Polar Bear Day
days.description: Raises awareness of the threats facing polar bears, including habitat loss from climate-driven sea ice decline.

days.title: World Wildlife Day
days.description: UN observance celebrating wild fauna and flora and raising awareness of the many benefits they provide to people.

days.title: World Frog Day
days.description: Highlights the global amphibian crisis — frogs are among the most threatened vertebrate groups, with over a third of species at risk.

days.title: World Water Day
days.description: UN annual observance focusing on the importance of freshwater and the 2.2 billion people living without access to safe water.

days.title: Earth Day
days.description: Annual event demonstrating support for environmental protection, first held on April 22, 1970, now marked in over 193 countries.

days.title: World Penguin Day
days.description: Coincides with the annual northward migration of Adélie penguins past the McMurdo Station research base in Antarctica.

days.title: World Bee Day
days.description: UN day raising awareness of the essential role bees and other pollinators play in keeping people and the planet healthy.

days.title: International Day for Biological Diversity
days.description: UN day to increase understanding and awareness of biodiversity issues, marking the date the Convention on Biological Diversity opened for signature.

days.title: World Turtle Day
days.description: Annual observance to bring attention to and increase knowledge of and respect for turtles and tortoises, and their vanishing habitats.

days.title: World Environment Day
days.description: The UN's principal vehicle for encouraging awareness and action for the protection of the environment, celebrated since 1974.

days.title: World Ocean Day
days.description: UN observance reminding every person of the major role the oceans have in everyday life — they are the lungs of our planet.

days.title: World Giraffe Day
days.description: Annual event by the Giraffe Conservation Foundation to celebrate the tallest animal on Earth and raise awareness of its silent extinction.

days.title: International Tiger Day
days.description: Annual event to raise awareness for tiger conservation, established at the 2010 Saint Petersburg Tiger Summit after wild tiger numbers fell to ~3,200.

days.title: World Lion Day
days.description: Annual campaign to raise awareness of the plight of lions, whose African population has declined 43% over the past 21 years.

days.title: World Elephant Day
days.description: International event dedicated to the preservation and protection of the world's elephants, launched in 2012 by Patricia Sims.

days.title: World Rhino Day
days.description: WWF South Africa initiative celebrating all five rhinoceros species and raising awareness of the threats they face from poaching.

days.title: World Gorilla Day
days.description: Annual celebration to raise awareness and encourage support for gorilla conservation efforts across Africa.

days.title: World Animal Day
days.description: International day of action for animal rights and welfare, held on the feast day of Francis of Assisi, patron saint of animals.

days.title: International Snow Leopard Day
days.description: Established at the 2013 Bishkek Declaration by 12 range countries to protect snow leopards and their high-mountain habitats.

days.title: Wildlife Conservation Day
days.description: Raises awareness about wildlife crime and threats to biodiversity, first proclaimed by U.S. Secretary of State Hillary Clinton in 2012.
